body:
Maintain garage door tracks by keeping them clean and dry, as lubrication causes dirt buildup that leads to sticking. Regularly wipe tracks with a damp cloth to remove debris, and ensure rollers spin freely by applying lubricant only to the roller bearings, not the track itself. [Garage Door Getting Louder? This is Why and How To Fix It!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G8oUB0C6At8&vl=en&t=229)[How to Lubricate a Garage Door: Complete Guide](https://www.crcindustries.com/blog/how-to-lubricate-a-garage-door/)

**Key Maintenance Steps to Prevent Sticking:**

  * **Keep Tracks Dry:** Never lubricate the tracks; they must remain clean and dry, as lubricant attracts dirt, creating sticky grime.
  * **Clean Regularly:** Use a damp cloth to remove dirt, grease, and debris from the inside of the tracks.
  * **Lubricate Moving Parts:** Use a [silicone-based spray or white lithium grease](https://www.crcindustries.com/blog/how-to-lubricate-a-garage-door/) on roller bearings, hinges, springs, and bearings at least once a year to keep them moving smoothly.
  * **Check for Obstructions:** Ensure no screws or bolts are loose, and the tracks are properly aligned and free of obstructions.
  * **Clean Rollers:** Use a rag to clean the roller wheels, but avoid spraying them directly with lubricant to prevent slipping. [What to Use and Where to Lubricate Garage Doors](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SKceowAb26w)[Garage Door Getting Louder? This is Why and How To Fix It!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G8oUB0C6At8&vl=en&t=229)[How to Lubricate a Garage Door: Complete Guide](https://www.crcindustries.com/blog/how-to-lubricate-a-garage-door/)[How to Lubricate a Garage Door + Best Garage Door Lubricants](https://artisandoorworks.com/blog/how-to-lubricate-a-garage-door-so-it-isnt-noisy/)[Everything You Should Know About a Garage Door Track](https://rainierdoor.com/blog/garage-door-track/)

This video shows you which parts of your garage door to lubricate for optimal performance:

If the door still sticks, inspect the rollers for damage and ensure they are not sliding instead of rolling.

Would you like to know how to fix a **misaligned** track, **replace damaged rollers** , or find the **best lubricant brands** to use?
